description: A Japanese martial art emphasizing powerful strikes, precise blocks, and disciplined kata. Develops explosive speed, balance, and mental focus through traditional techniques.

movements.name: Mae Geri (Front Kick)

commonMistakes.description: Leaning backward during the kick, reducing power and exposing the body.
Not retracting the kicking leg quickly after extension, leaving it vulnerable.

movements.description: A straight front kick using the ball of the foot to target the midsection or face. The chambering and snapping motion develops hip flexibility and explosive leg speed.

movements.name: Mawashi Geri (Roundhouse Kick)

commonMistakes.description: Dropping the guard hand on the kicking side, leaving the head open to counterattack.
Insufficient hip rotation, reducing reach and impact power of the kick.

movements.description: A circular kick sweeping horizontally to strike with the instep or ball of the foot. Full hip rotation drives the power behind this versatile and widely-used technique.

movements.name: Gyaku Zuki (Reverse Punch)

commonMistakes.description: Punching with the arm only and not engaging hip rotation, resulting in weak technique.
Raising the shoulder during the punch instead of keeping it relaxed and down.

movements.description: A powerful straight punch from the rear hand using full hip rotation. Considered the cornerstone of karate striking, generating force from the entire body's kinetic chain.

movements.name: Oi Zuki (Lunge Punch)

commonMistakes.description: Bobbing the head up and down during the stepping motion, telegraphing the attack.
Landing with the front foot before the punch arrives, breaking the kime and losing power.

movements.description: A stepping punch where the strike lands simultaneously with the forward foot. Combines forward momentum with punching power for a committed, aggressive attack.

movements.name: Shuto Uke (Knife Hand Block)

commonMistakes.description: Bending the elbow too much on the blocking arm, reducing the effective blocking area.
Neglecting to pull the opposite hand back to the hip, losing the body's counter-balance and power.

movements.description: A sweeping block using the edge of the open hand to deflect attacks. The technique simultaneously redirects the strike while positioning the other hand to counter.

movements.name: Ushiro Geri (Back Kick)

commonMistakes.description: Not looking over the shoulder to aim before firing the kick, resulting in poor accuracy.
Leaning too far forward to compensate for balance, which telegraphs the kick and reduces power.

movements.description: A rear-directed kick using the heel with devastating penetrating power. Requires a look-back pivot to aim, developing exceptional balance and spatial awareness.

movements.name: Jodan Uke (Upper Block)

commonMistakes.description: Blocking too far out from the head, reducing protection and losing structural integrity.

movements.description: A rising forearm block that deflects high attacks away from the head. One of the first defensive techniques taught, forming the bedrock of karate's blocking system.

movements.name: Morote Uke (Augmented Block)

commonMistakes.description: Failing to rotate the hips into the block, making it arm-only and easily overwhelmed.
Placing the augmenting fist too high on the blocking arm, reducing leverage.

movements.description: A reinforced block where the second arm braces the primary blocking arm for maximum power. Used against heavy attacks where a single-arm block would be overwhelmed.

movements.name: Gedan Barai (Downward Block)

commonMistakes.description: Sweeping the blocking arm too far across the centerline, leaving the opposite side of the body unprotected.
Dropping the elbow below the hip on completion, weakening the block's structural endpoint.

movements.description: A sweeping downward block that deflects low kicks and punches from the lower gates. Often used as the opening technique in kata sequences to clear an attacking limb.

movements.name: Yoko Geri Keage (Side Snap Kick)

commonMistakes.description: Failing to raise the knee to full chamber height before snapping, which reduces range and makes the kick easy to predict.
Allowing the support hip to drop during execution, shifting the kicking arc downward and reducing target height.

movements.description: A sharp snapping side kick using the blade of the foot at close range. The quick chamber-and-snap motion targets the ribs, solar plexus, or groin with precision.

movements.name: Uchi Uke (Inside Block)

commonMistakes.description: Pulling the blocking arm too far across the body's centerline, creating a gap in the guard on the opposite side.
Not pivoting the hips to add power to the block, leaving it as an arm-only deflection.

movements.description: An inward sweeping forearm block that deflects mid-level attacks across the body. The forearm rotates at contact to add a redirecting quality to the deflection.

movements.name: Kizami Zuki (Jab Punch)

commonMistakes.description: Reaching forward with the shoulder to gain extra range, telegraphing the attack and compromising the shoulder joint.
Failing to snap the fist back to guard immediately after impact, leaving the arm extended and exposed to a grab or counter.

movements.description: A quick lead-hand jab that closes distance and sets up combinations. Speed and timing are prioritized over power, making it a critical distancing and disruption tool.
